evidence: R2 running-config: interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1 has Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:speed value-100 and Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:duplex = "half"; negotiation auto = false (get_running_config:router2).
R1 running-config: interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1 has Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:negotiation = { "auto": true } (get_running_config:router1).
R1 interface status: admin_state=up oper_state=up speed=102400000 input_errors=57 output_errors=0 (get_interface_status:router1:GigabitEthernet0/0/1).
R2 interface status: admin_state=up oper_state=up speed=102400000 input_errors=59 output_errors=0 (get_interface_status:router2:GigabitEthernet0/0/1).
R1 running-config: "Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:negotiation": { "auto": true } on interface GigabitEthernet0/0/1 (get_running_config:router1)
R1 interface status: admin_state=up, oper_state=up, speed=102400000, input_errors=57 (get_interface_status:router1:GigabitEthernet0/0/1)
R2 interface status: admin_state=up, oper_state=up, speed=102400000, input_errors=59 (get_interface_status:router2:GigabitEthernet0/0/1)
Interface duplex not reported by status (duplex=null), consistent with one side forced and the other auto-negotiating (get_interface_status outputs)

plan_history.intent_description: Input errors on GigabitEthernet0/0/1 on router1 and router2 caused by a duplex/speed negotiation mismatch; align duplex/speed to restore link-layer health (set R2 to auto-negotiation to match R1).
plan_history.problem: Router2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is configured fixed at 100 Mbps, half-duplex while Router1 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is auto-negotiating; this mismatch is generating input_errors on both interfaces.
plan_history.fix_summary: Change router2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 to use auto-negotiation (speed=auto, duplex=auto) to match router1.

plan_history.intent_description: Input errors on GigabitEthernet0/0/1 on router2 caused by a speed/duplex negotiation mismatch with router1; restore link-layer health by configuring router2's GigabitEthernet0/0/1 to use auto-negotiation (speed/duplex auto).
plan_history.problem: GigabitEthernet0/0/1 on router2 is manually configured for 100 Mbps, half-duplex with auto-negotiation disabled while router1 is set to auto-negotiation, causing input errors and degraded link-layer performance.
plan_history.fix_summary: Enable auto-negotiation on router2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 so speed and duplex are negotiated automatically to match router1.

remaining_issues: No post-change interface status for GigabitEthernet0/0/1 on R2 (and no updated status from R1) to confirm the link actually re-negotiated to matching speed/duplex and that the input error counters cleared.
Cannot confirm whether duplex/speed are now matched on both ends or whether the link-layer performance (input errors) improved after the config change.
